text: Metformin is an oral medicine used to improve blood sugar control in adults with type 2 diabetes. It is commonly used together with diet and exercise and is often a first-line treatment.
Insulin is a hormone therapy used to lower blood glucose. It is required for type 1 diabetes and may also be used in type 2 diabetes when other treatments are not enough.
Amoxicillin is a penicillin antibiotic used to treat certain bacterial infections. It is not used to treat viral infections such as the common cold.
Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever. It can irritate the stomach and should be used carefully in some patients.
Hypertension means persistently elevated blood pressure. Treatment may include lifestyle changes and antihypertensive medications depending on severity and overall cardiovascular risk.
